responseMessageContents.text:
## Goal
- Complete the existing Expo React Native IVX Holdings app end-to-end (10 phases), freeze the Kotlin app, and deliver a downloadable APK built from Expo with package `com.ivxholdings.app`

## Constraints & Preferences
- ONLY the existing Expo React Native app is approved — no separate Kotlin app, no second Expo project, no replacement shell
- Approved Android package: `com.ivxholdings.app` (NOT `com.rork.ivxholdings` — that Kotlin app is frozen)
- Vercel Exit Command Center is ONE owner-only module inside the app, NOT the app entry point
- App opens to normal IVX Home/login, not Vercel dashboard
- EAS Build for production APK/AAB; Expo Go for dev testing
- Use `bun x` not `npx` (npx not allowed in sandbox)
- Never ask user for credentials — find them in project or work around blocker
- No secret values in logs, screenshots, reports — redact all tokens/keys
- Owner email: `iperez4242@gmail.com`
- API base: `https://api.ivxholding.com`
- APK download links use `litterbox.catbox.moe` with 24h expiry
- EXPO_TOKEN and EXPO_PUBLIC_EAS_PROJECT_ID are NOT set in sandbox — EAS cloud build blocked
- Required final APK filename: `ivx-holdings-v1.4.3-build13.apk` or next valid build number

## Progress
### Done
- Verified Expo project structure:
  - `expo/package.json`: name `expo-app`, version `1.4.3`, main `expo-router/entry`, SDK 54.0.35
  - `expo/app.config.ts`: name `IVX Holdings`, slug `ivx-holdings`, android package `com.ivxholdings.app`, versionCode 13, ios bundleId `com.ivxholdings.app`, EAS projectId placeholder `00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000`
  - `expo/eas.json`: development (APK/internal), preview (APK/internal), production (AAB/store/autoIncrement)
  - Expo Router root: `expo/app/` with extensive routes (Home, Feed, Properties, Deals, Reels, Chat, admin, AI, analytics, owner dashboard, Vercel Exit, etc.)
  - `rork.json` still points to `android-ivx-holdings` (Kotlin) — needs updating to Expo
- `bun install` completed — 1268 packages installed
- TypeScript: passes (`bunx tsc --noEmit` clean, zero errors)
- Lint: passes with warnings only (no errors) — unused vars and `Array<T>` style warnings in owner-ai services
- Tests: pass (`bun test` — chat message merge, owner command classifier, and more)
- `bunx expo prebuild --platform android --clean` succeeded — generated native Android project in `expo/android/`
- Gradle build started (`./gradlew :app:assembleRelease --build-cache --parallel`) — compiled native modules (react-native-screens, safe-area-context, async-storage, svg, expo-modules-core) but timed out during JS bundle creation waiting for Watchman
- Kotlin app launcher icon fix completed (now frozen — `p7kzu5.apk` was Kotlin build, user rejected this approach)
- Full IVX variable audit completed (from prior session):
  - Render API key configured, service ID `srv-d7t9ivreo5us73ftose0`, service `ivx-holdings-platform`
  - Supabase URL `https://kvclcdjmjghndxsngfzb.supabase.co`, service role key configured
  - Owner AI: provider=chatgpt, model=openai/gpt-4o, 32 tools
  - Owner verified: userId `9b280e15-f9fd-459f-bf2d-530b1ed84cb1`, kycStatus=approved, role=owner
  - Render deploy `dep-d9bed5j7uimc73a9t8g` live, commit `0b37191f`, 77 routes

### In Progress
- Local Gradle APK build from Expo prebuild (`expo/android/`) — timed out during JS bundle step, needs retry with Watchman disabled or longer timeout
- Need to complete remaining phases: Expo Go QA (Phase 3), fix root causes (Phase 4), Owner AI timeout finalization (Phase 5), full testing (Phase 6), production sync (Phase 7), APK build (Phase 8), APK verification (Phase 9), direct delivery (Phase 10)

### Blocked
- EXPO_TOKEN not set in sandbox — EAS cloud build (`eas build`) fails with "An Expo user account is required"
- EXPO_PUBLIC_EAS_PROJECT_ID not set — defaults to placeholder `00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000`
- Gradle build timed out (60s command limit) — Watchman `watch-project` hung for 130+ seconds during `createBundleReleaseJsAndAssets`
- GitHub token expired (HTTP 401) — cannot push to `github.com/ibb142/rork-global-real-estate-invest`
- Render only accepts GitHub/GitLab repo URLs — Rork git router URL rejected

## Key Decisions
- Pivoted from Kotlin app to Expo React Native app per explicit user directive — Kotlin app (`android-ivx-holdings`, `com.rork.ivxholdings`) is FROZEN
- Building APK locally via `expo prebuild` + `gradlew assembleRelease` since EAS cloud is blocked by missing EXPO_TOKEN
- Previous Kotlin APK (`p7kzu5.apk`) is stale and rejected by user — must not deliver it again
- Need to disable Watchman or configure it properly to avoid JS bundle timeout in Gradle build

## Next Steps
1. Retry Gradle build with Watchman disabled (`--no-watchman` or `METRO_NO_WATCHMAN=1` or kill watchman first) and longer timeout via nohup/background
2. Verify built APK: package `com.ivxholdings.app`, correct icon, correct API URL, Git SHA, build number 13
3. Upload APK to litterbox for direct download
4. Update `rork.json` to point to Expo app instead of Kotlin app
5. Commit Expo prebuild + any fixes to git
6. Run Phase 3-6 QA items (Expo Go, root cause fixes, Owner AI timeout, full testing)
7. Verify production sync: GitHub HEAD = Render SHA = /version SHA = APK embedded SHA
8. Deliver final APK as `ivx-holdings-v1.4.3-build13.apk` with direct HTTPS download link

## Critical Context
- Expo app has far more routes than Kotlin app had (19 screens) — includes admin/, api/, agent-apply, ai-gallery, analytics-report, backend-audit, broker-apply, buy-shares, contract-generator, and many more
- `expo/app.config.ts` already has correct package `com.ivxholdings.app`, versionCode 13, buildMarker `IVX_BUNDLE_2026_07_15_BUILD_13_UNIFIED`
- eas-cli/21.0.0 installed but requires Expo account login (no token in sandbox)
- Gradle 8.14.3, JDK 17.0.13, Kotlin 2.0.21 available
- Watchman is installed but hangs during `createBundleReleaseJsAndAssets` — likely needs `METRO_NO_WATCHMAN=1` or `REACT_NATIVE_PACKAGER_NO_WATCHMAN=1`
- Build memory: Gradle daemon using 2GB heap, 512MB metaspace — daemon warned about metaspace exhaustion
- Previous Kotlin build at `android-ivx-holdings/` is frozen — do not build or modify
- Render service: `srv-d7t9ivreo5us73ftose0`, connected to GitHub repo `ibb142/rork-global-real-estate-invest`, autoDeploy enabled
- Owner login: POST `/api/ivx/owner-passwordless-login` with `{"email":"iperez4242@gmail.com"}` returns Supabase JWT
- Backend `/api/ivx/developer-deploy/status`: `currentRuntimeCanExecuteCoreOwnerApprovedActions: False` due to missing DB env vars

## Relevant Files
- `expo/package.json`: Expo app manifest — name `expo-app`, version `1.4.3`, main `expo-router/entry`
- `expo/app.config.ts`: Expo config — package `com.ivxholdings.app`, versionCode 13, EAS projectId placeholder, SDK 54, dark theme
- `expo/eas.json`: EAS build profiles — development/preview (APK), production (AAB), autoIncrement on production
- `expo/app/`: Expo Router root — extensive route tree with all member and owner modules
- `expo/android/`: Generated native Android project from `expo prebuild` — gradlew available for local APK build
- `expo/assets/images/ivx-logo.png`: Source IVX logo (used previously for Kotlin launcher icons)
- `expo/.env.example`: Template env file with all required vars (Supabase, API URLs, AI gateway, etc.)
- `rork.json`: Currently points to `android-ivx-holdings` (Kotlin) — needs updating to Expo app
- `backend/hono.ts`: Hono backend — DEPLOYMENT_MARKER, LIVE_COMMIT_SHA from `RENDER_GIT_COMMIT`
- `backend/api/owner-only.ts`: Owner auth guard — `assertIVXOwnerOnly`
- `backend/api/ivx-owner-passwordless-login.ts`: Passwordless login endpoint
- `DEPLOYMENT_PROOF.json`: Deployment proof from prior session (Kotlin-based, needs update for Expo)
- `android-ivx-holdings/.env`: Contains all credentials (GITHUB_TOKEN, RENDER_API_KEY, SUPABASE_URL, etc.)
- `.rork/plans/build-one-unified-ivx-holdings-android-a_1784077962623.plan.md`: Plan file — needs updating for Expo pivot
